items.headline: Library parcel tax rates to increase for FY 2026-27
items.summary: San José City Council approved an increase to library parcel tax rates for fiscal year 2026-27. Property owners will see higher taxes on their bills to fund library operations.

items.headline: Eight new homes proposed on Wolfe Road single-family lot
items.summary: A special development permit and tentative map filed to demolish one house at 781 S Wolfe Road and build eight two-story single-family dwellings on the 0.69-acre site. The project includes subdividing the property into nine lots with a 65.8% floor area ratio. Planning Commission scheduled a study session to review the related applications on June 8.

items.headline: 136-unit apartment complex permitted on Yoshino Way
items.summary: Trimble Modular Apts receives permit for 136 apartments and a separate parking structure (combined ~$27M) in the Trimble area. This is the largest housing project in this permit batch and adds meaningful rental capacity to San José's housing stock.

items.headline: Historic house on Crescent Avenue to anchor four-townhome development
items.summary: A resource alteration permit for 434 Crescent Avenue proposes retaining a 960-square-foot historic house while adding four three-story townhomes in the rear. The Heritage Preservation Commission continued the item to July 1, 2026.

items.headline: November 2026 municipal election called for three council seats
items.summary: Los Gatos will hold a general municipal election on November 3, 2026, to fill three City Council positions. This is a routine election cycle notification.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 residential development on Boulevard
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution approving the final map for Tract 10675, a residential subdivision located on Boulevard. Final tract map approval clears the way for the project to move toward construction.

items.headline: FY 2026-27 Community Development Block Grant allocation
items.summary: City Council considered how to allocate federal Community Development Block Grant funds for the coming fiscal year. The agenda item was cut off, so specific funding amounts and projects are not available in the source material.
